(1) Founders and Key Team: Name – Title Noah Martins — CEO One-line career summary; track record: Growth @ GameRamp; previously cofounder/CEO @ Fijit; GM @ Loop AI; Producer @ Gajabuja Games Functional responsibility: overall leadership, growth, and product direction Equity % / commitment / location: not disclosed Relevance: prior startup and growth leadership; experience across gaming and AI-adjacent teams Kartik Karia — COO One-line career summary; track record: Prev: cofounder/CGO @ Fijit Functional responsibility: operations, go-to-market execution Equity % / commitment / location: not disclosed Relevance: previous founder/CGO experience relevant to GTM and operations Mohit Kumar — CTO One-line career summary; track record: Senior SDE at Fijit, Streak AI; Previously built 30+ games at Fijit and scaled to 100K+ users Functional responsibility: technology development and architecture; AI/ingestion pipeline Equity % / commitment / location: not disclosed Relevance: strong technical background with gaming/AI-scale experience
Company Snapshot: Problem & solution Reading lacks daily feedback; progress is invisible, hurting motivation. Existing tools track books or time, not the habit. Bookmarkk positions as the “Strava for books” with AI-driven quizzes, social features, and an identity/insights layer to turn reading into a measurable habit. Ideal customer profile and pricing Pricing tested: $5 per month (24 paid users in initial test). Target segments implied: high‑intent reade...

Boost <> @Aakash / @Sudipto / @Naviya Singh 2025-07-09: Please find below the views and meeting notes from our chat with the Founders of Boost Robotics. Next steps here include making them meet key folks from the DeVC collective, forming a WhatsApp group for the same shortly. Vault links to the following: - Pitch Deck - Research Papers Views Dada (Strong Maybe): Spiky: 1 + Continue to like the founders - spiky, YC archetype. +/? Very early in the product and revenue journey and will have their learning curve on hiring and GTM. + However, spiky enough for us to do a small cheque through DeVC and continue to track Cash (Maybe+): Spiky: 0.5 + Like the guys, good pedigree + Smart GTM strategy: wedge into real-world need (data centers), expand from there. + Will surround them with DeVC collective folks. ? Not in the bay area, going to be hard for them to raise. ? Unsure of how to benchmark and calibrate. NS (Strong Maybe): Spiky: 1 EF/FF: FF Founder Archetype: 8 FMF: 9 Thesis: 8 + Articulate, pedigreed (both founders), clear FMF. + Aligns with internal thesis of robots (non AGVs and AMRs) for specialized use cases. + Already have LOIs and pilot partners. + In the DeVC alley, will help us enter the CMU network. + Met Hans for the first time- liked him, clear tech spike. + Clear articulation of problem and product roadmap. + Both founders are customer backwards- rarity in robotics founders building in the US. Meeting Notes

Notes:: AS: NS (Strong Maybe) Spiky: 0.5 (Tech spike) EF/FF: FF Founder Archetype: 7 FMF: 8 Thesis: 7 + Strong pedigreed team with domain experience. + Already have ongoing paid pilots with Airbus, Safran, Ministry of Heavy Industries. ? Yash is scientist archetype, not articulate but possesses clear tech spike. ? How big of a market is this? Path to $100 M ARR is unclear. Meeting Notes Team - Abhishek Chopra (CEO and Co-Founder) - Rut Lineswala (Co-founder) - Team size: ~40 people Problem - Current simulation software extremely slow & computationally expensive - Engineers forced to choose between: - Example: Aircraft simulations take 6 months on 250,000 parallel computers - Algorithms haven’t changed since 1970s - GPUs underutilized (only 30-35% capability used in engine simulations) Product - BQFi: Backend engine for simulation software - Uses quantum-inspired algorithms on existing hardware - Key features: - Technical differentiator: Uses stochastic math & quantum-inspired gates - Can find global minima faster than genetic algorithms Go-to-market - Initial focus: Aerospace & Defense sector - GTM Strategy: Conference presentations, keynotes, booth setups - Position as backend engine for existing simulation software - Target customers: Airbus, Israel Aerospace, Air Force Research Lab - Future expansion planned into biotech, pharma, finance Traction - Major pilot with Airbus: - Pipeline includes: Funding - Currently raising seed round: - Series A metrics set:
Meeting Notes — Feb 20, 2026: 1. Reco product has taken off 2. Do follow up meeting next week 3. Feedback from CD and ID - why are they using it 4. Payment orchestrator isn't clicking

Ref from Priyans (CoFo): Context: Priyans was a CoFo of the Breethr founders in their prev company ActiveBuildings Notes: 1. Priyans is also involved in Breethr - more on back office 2. Priyans also runs an EV business - he is more focused there 3. Priyans and Abhinav met on BITS Goa campus in 2010 - filed patents together, research together and both decided to do something in hardware together 4. Pivoted to air quality in 2014 5. ActiveBuilding had a F&F round 6. Manav was hired around COVID (2020) when they were doing a Swiggy project - Manav had an inside sales background and was boots on ground in BLR 7. Abhinav and him are hands on founders 8. Peak was 30 employees in 3 cities 9. Peak revenue was ₹7 crore - ₹1 crore PAT 10. Sensor doesn't make as much money as solutions 11. Abhinav was great at B2B deals eg Reliance NetMeds 12. NetMeds warehouse - temperature and humidity monitoring 13. Abhinav used to crack deal and then Priyans would do implementation 14. Still has positive relationship with Abhinav 15. Pandemic era - got $50L from DOD for Kavach grant 16. PAPR - Priyans and Abhinav built this solution, but by then PPE was in the vogue, army wanted something more sturdier 17. PAPR journey gave them an idea of how solutions are built 18. Started as distributors of air purifiers 19. Simple product is easy, sexy product needs capex investment 20. Closed a ₹2 crore order during pandemic for MI team (for ActiveBuilding) 21. Call center incident - Disprin stock was still wit...

Evaluation Plan + Key Questions to answer: 1. What does the company do? 1. Do we (DeVC/Z47) have a thesis on this space? 1. What are the top 3 questions to diligence? 1. Who has piped this company to us? What are their views? 1. What do refs say about the founder? 1. What's the DeVC team views? 1. What are the Collective signals? 1. Why is this company likely to be special? 1. Who can help? Experts / Venture Partners? 1. Deal dynamics? Potential deal to do? ---

Attendees: Full Z47 team: Briskpe Views RC -Weak Maybe Archetype 3/4 +Liked Indu a little more than Aloke and Sanjay, FMF- Below 5 + More than expected amount of work on regulatory piece and customers. + Ahead in terms of reg thinking and execution compared to others. ? Bold choice on the segment → Feels like they’ve underestimated the challenges of the segment they are going after though. Not sure they’re fully cognizant of digital strengths of the other players / are thinking along those lines. Avi – Very easy pass Low Archetype, IFs + Aloke has some conviction. ? FMF is 0, Not my archetype → Indu is just words not content - Struggling to give a clincher reason why to pass , “there’s a few things such as the teams background that makes you pause they say some of the right things but it never really ties together.” ?Complete IFs stumbling from one insight to another. ? Not sure if the refs on Sanjay are high quality. (Would not give any weight to Gaurav Chopra’s ref either) → Navin might have a positive view but he likes these kind of founding team. ? Sanjay is someone who has never compounded in life, and everything he has done post HDFC (Venture Studio) is scattered → This journey also seems scattered. VV-Weak maybe Insight / spike missing / edge missing. Depth in biz model / product etc Sanjay more thoughtful than I gave him credit for Team, Talent, Cost/frugality, pivot at the right time, bend at the right time 7 days digital journey vs offline; more digital thinking tha...

RM notes: Next steps - BRC w/ DoWhile AI CoFo from RM + 2PE with Kinzy & Bunny with RA & Mohit Notes 1. Naman Jain - Elder brother 1. Nishchal Jain - Younger brother 1. Startup journey 1. DoWhile AI 1. Tinkering together post SPC and LossFunk 1. Buny 1. Why pendant? 1. Started to spend too much time on hardware - “juice is in AI” 1. How did walkie talkie come about? 1. Demo video 1. Cash Q: Why not go full fictional characters like EMoMee? 1. Cash Q: Hardware - why not make it a analogue kid mobile? 1. What are you hiring for? 1. Parent mobile 1. Initial market (?) 1. Comps: 1. Can offer personalized learning storybooks | learning activities → personalized 1. Customers 1. Learning from Osmo founder - keep founder healthy & high 1. $150 retail price for hardware 1. Technology
RM Notes: 1. Shubham knows Deepak 1. Knows Aniket Kamte from Nexus VP 1. Left Google because he wanted to take some risk 1. Joined DoWhile AI when it was called Collective AI 1. v/s othr companies 1. Mandalin is his current co 1. Convinction / motivation lacking at the firm - didn’t have as many wins 1. Going to meet them in SF 1. Come a long way - motivated now versus before 1. He feels choice of market isn’t something he would do 1. They need a CTO/ VPEng
Cash reflections -  Weak Maybe. Community cheque at best.: 1. Pure inside out FFs with no clear consumer spike 1. Have built and landed on a much better form factor (hardware and software) in the entire audio ai toy for kids than all other teams we hav...

RM reflections - dead center Maybe | 0.5% cheque = $150K IFF we go ahead w/ this: ++ Pedigreed team given 4 YOE @ Loom - worked closely w/ founders on the Loom AI launch ++ Thesis (MUCH more than Cash & KB): For me, this it Limitless 1.0 i.e. agent running in the background learning everything about you ++ Design principle struck a deep chord with me: Voice to be used for editing the “first draft” created by the Caddy Agent ++ Good set of learnings captured by them - Enterprise plan beyond billing consolidation & SSO ++ Do sense they have GTM chops; not just Product builders - but yet to be proven out / demonstrated ?? Worried that they will repeat the same Loom mistakes i.e. build a horizontal OS level product which will struggle to capture Enterprise revenue ?? Worried that their landing wedge is dictation - didn’t hear anything about actions - therefore, Wispr Flow is the competition ?? Underestimating the amount of configuration & customization needed to make even dictation work Mid Feb ‘26 launch Key Qs - business 1. Why choose to go after this problem? 1. Why standalone app & not OS level solution? 1. What do “actions” require v/s Wispr which is transcribing today
RM’s suggestion = 0.5% ownership i.e. $150K cheque 2025-12-12T22:39:00.000+05:30: BRC w/ Joe (Loom CoFo) took me from Maybe to SM - sticking to the 0.5% ownership target i.e. $150K cheque size Why $150K? - Connor (founder we are underwriting) is Spiky = 1 - rapid career trajectory from intern to Eng to PM to G...
